title: Lobelia koolauensis
intro: Lobelia koolauensis, also known as Koolau lobelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to Hawaii. Traditionally, Lobelia koolauensis has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an expectorant and cough suppressant, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function. The bioactive constituents of Lobelia koolauensis include lobeline, lobelanine, and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lobelia koolauensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and relieve coughs and congestion.
